text: Access Control Standard Role-based access reviews are performed every 90 days by system owners and security operations. Multi-factor authentication is mandatory for all admin and support-console accounts. Privileged sessions must be logged and retained for at least 400 days. Temporary elevated access expires automatically after 8 hours unless renewed with manager approval.

Billing Dispute Playbook All invoice disputes must be acknowledged within 1 business day. Standard-priority disputes have a target resolution SLA of 3 business days after acknowledgement. Enterprise-priority disputes must be resolved within 24 hours. Required evidence for triage includes invoice ID, transaction reference, and the disputed contract line item. If a dispute remains unresolved after the SLA window, it is escalated to finance-oncall and customer success leadership in the same day.

Data Retention and Deletion Policy Customer support tickets are retained for 24 months and then archived. Audit logs are retained for 400 days to support security and compliance investigations. Customer deletion requests must be fully completed within 30 days of verified request receipt. Backup snapshots are encrypted and rotated every 14 days.

Incident Response SLA Severity 1 incidents require acknowledgement within 15 minutes and a customer-facing workaround within 4 hours. The formal root cause analysis document is due within 3 business days of service restoration. Severity 2 incidents require acknowledgement within 1 hour and restoration within 1 business day. Post-incident actions are tracked in the reliability backlog until closure.

Customer Onboarding Handover A solution architect handover must occur within the first 7 days after contract signature. The customer success plan is reviewed monthly with goals, blockers, and adoption metrics. For enterprise customers, executive checkpoints are scheduled every quarter. Open onboarding risks are reviewed weekly until go-live is complete.
